description: Implement the two user-facing endpoints required for GitHub-first registration and profile retrieval: POST /api/v1/registrations and GET /api/v1/me. The registration endpoint lives in user-service and is responsible for upserting user_profile, creating the GitHub external_identity snapshot, assigning initial roles, generating a correlation ID, writing a UserRegisteredV1 outbox event, and synchronously calling onboarding-service to create the onboarding_request with its initial steps. The GET /api/v1/me endpoint returns the internal user profile with optional linked identities and active onboarding summary. All request and response shapes must match docs/rest-api-reference.md exactly. All error responses must use the standard error envelope. Exit criteria: a GitHub-authenticated frontend can POST a registration and receive a 201 with userId, onboardingRequestId, correlationId, and steps[]; API responses match the spec; validation and error envelopes are consistent.
functionality: register after completing GitHub OAuth in the frontend and immediately receive an onboarding request ID and step status to track provisioning progress
benefit: the frontend can start a user session, submit identity data once, and begin polling onboarding state without any manual backend intervention

stories.summary: Registration DTO, Bean Validation, and global error envelope
stories.description: Define the RegistrationRequest and RegistrationResponse DTOs in <backend/user-service/> and wire a global exception handler that converts validation failures and domain exceptions into the standard error envelope defined in <docs/rest-api-reference.md>. The error envelope has four fields: code, message, correlationId, and details. This handler must be in place before the controller is wired so all error paths are consistently formatted from the start.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Missing required field returns 400 with error envelope
acceptanceCriteria.given: a POST /api/v1/registrations request body that omits the required githubUserId field
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is HTTP 400 with a JSON body containing code, message, correlationId, and details fields matching the error envelope schema

acceptanceCriteria.given: a valid registration has been processed
acceptanceCriteria.then: userId, onboardingRequestId, status, correlationId, and steps[] are all present with correct types

acceptanceCriteria.given: the registration service throws an unexpected RuntimeException
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is HTTP 500 with the standard error envelope and no stack trace in the body

tasks.summary: Create RegistrationRequest DTO with validation annotations
tasks.description: Add xyz.catuns.onboarding.user.api.dto.RegistrationRequest with fields githubUserId (@NotBlank), githubLogin (@NotBlank), primaryEmail (@Email, nullable), displayName (@NotBlank), avatarUrl (nullable), and roleKeys (List<String>, nullable). Annotate the class with @Valid for Spring MVC binding.

Add xyz.catuns.onboarding.user.api.dto.RegistrationResponse with fields userId (UUID), onboardingRequestId (UUID), status (String), correlationId (UUID), and steps (List<StepSummaryDto>). Add StepSummaryDto with type and state fields.

Add xyz.catuns.onboarding.user.api.dto.ErrorResponse with fields code (String), message (String), correlationId (UUID), and details (Map<String, Object>). This DTO is used by all error handlers across user-service.

Add xyz.catuns.onboarding.user.api.GlobalExceptionHandler handling: MethodArgumentNotValidException -> 400, ConstraintViolationException -> 400, DuplicateRegistrationException -> 409, NoSuchElementException -> 404, and all unhandled Throwable -> 500. Each handler builds an ErrorResponse. Extract correlationId from MDC when available.

Add MockMvc-based tests verifying that each exception type produces the correct HTTP status and that the response body matches the error envelope shape. Test at least: missing field -> 400, duplicate registration -> 409, unhandled exception -> 500.

stories.summary: UserRegistrationService — user profile upsert and GitHub identity snapshot
stories.description: Implement the core registration domain logic in <backend/user-service/>. The service must: look up an existing user_profile by githubUserId via external_identity (upsert semantics — re-registration of an active user raises DuplicateRegistrationException); create user_profile if new; create or update external_identity with the GitHub snapshot fields (githubUserId, githubLogin, primaryEmail, displayName, avatarUrl, profile_json); assign AppRole records from the provided roleKeys, resolving them from the app_role table; generate a UUID correlation ID; and write an unpublished UserRegisteredV1 outbox_event row in the same transaction. The Kafka relay is out of scope for Phase 2 and will be added in Phase 4.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: New user registration creates profile and identity
acceptanceCriteria.given: no user_profile exists for the provided githubUserId
acceptanceCriteria.then: a new user_profile row and an external_identity row with is_primary=true are persisted in the same transaction

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user_profile with status ACTIVE already exists for the provided githubUserId
acceptanceCriteria.when: UserRegistrationService.register() is called again for the same githubUserId
acceptanceCriteria.then: DuplicateRegistrationException is thrown and no new rows are created

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Roles are resolved and assigned from roleKeys
acceptanceCriteria.given: the request includes roleKeys ["STUDENT"]
acceptanceCriteria.then: a user_role_assignment row linking the user_profile to the STUDENT AppRole is created

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Outbox event is written in the same transaction
acceptanceCriteria.given: a registration succeeds
acceptanceCriteria.then: an outbox_event row with event_type UserRegisteredV1 and published=false exists in the same database

tasks.description: Create @Service xyz.catuns.onboarding.user.service.UserRegistrationService with a @Transactional register(RegistrationRequest) method. Resolve existing identity via ExternalIdentityRepository.findByProviderKeyAndExternalUserId. Throw DuplicateRegistrationException if user_profile.status is ACTIVE. Otherwise create UserProfile (status=PENDING_ONBOARDING), ExternalIdentity, UserRoleAssignment rows, and an OutboxEvent.

Add xyz.catuns.onboarding.user.service.DuplicateRegistrationException as an unchecked exception with a field for the conflicting githubUserId. This is mapped to HTTP 409 by the GlobalExceptionHandler.

Add a UserRegisteredV1PayloadBuilder that constructs the JSON payload for the outbox_event row. Fields: userId, githubUserId, githubLogin, primaryEmail, correlationId, roleKeys, registeredAt. Serialize to a JSON string for the outbox_event.payload column.

Add integration tests using Testcontainers verifying: new user creates profile + identity + outbox row; duplicate active user raises exception; roles are assigned from roleKeys; correlation ID is non-null on every registration.

stories.summary: POST /api/v1/registrations controller and synchronous onboarding-service call
stories.description: Implement the RegistrationController in <backend/user-service/> that handles POST /api/v1/registrations. After UserRegistrationService persists the user, the controller must synchronously call an internal onboarding-service endpoint to create the onboarding_request and initial steps, then compose the HTTP 201 response from both results. Use Spring's RestClient (or WebClient) configured with the onboarding-service base URL from application properties. If the onboarding-service call fails, return HTTP 503 per the error handling defined in <docs/rest-api-reference.md>. The correlation ID generated during user creation must be forwarded as an X-Correlation-ID request header on the internal call.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a valid RegistrationRequest for a new GitHub user
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is HTTP 201 with userId, onboardingRequestId, status IN_PROGRESS, correlationId, and a steps[] array containing IDENTITY_CORRELATION, GITHUB_TEAM_PROVISIONING, and JIRA_GROUP_PROVISIONING each with state PENDING

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user_profile is already ACTIVE for the provided githubUserId
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is HTTP 409 with the standard error envelope

acceptanceCriteria.given: the onboarding-service internal endpoint is unreachable
acceptanceCriteria.when: POST /api/v1/registrations is called with a valid payload
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is HTTP 503 with the standard error envelope and the user-service transaction has already committed the user_profile and outbox_event

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Correlation ID appears in the response
acceptanceCriteria.given: a successful registration
acceptanceCriteria.then: the correlationId field is a non-null UUID matching the value forwarded to onboarding-service

tasks.description: Add @RestController xyz.catuns.onboarding.user.api.RegistrationController with @PostMapping("/api/v1/registrations"). Call UserRegistrationService then OnboardingServiceClient. Map the results to RegistrationResponse and return ResponseEntity.status(201).body(response).

Add xyz.catuns.onboarding.user.client.OnboardingServiceClient using Spring RestClient. Expose a createOnboardingRequest(UUID userId, UUID correlationId, List<String> roleKeys) method that POSTs to the onboarding-service internal endpoint and returns an OnboardingInitResponse. Wrap connectivity errors in OnboardingServiceUnavailableException mapped to 503.

tasks.summary: Configure onboarding-service base URL in application properties
tasks.description: Add onboarding-service.base-url property to application.yaml and application-local.yaml. Wire it into OnboardingServiceClient via @Value or AppProperties. Add the property to frontend/apps/web/.env.example equivalent for local dev.

tasks.summary: Add X-Correlation-ID header propagation to RestClient
tasks.description: Configure the RestClient bean to attach the current correlation ID as X-Correlation-ID on all outbound calls to onboarding-service. Read the correlation ID from MDC so it is always available within a request context.

tasks.summary: Write MockMvc integration tests for RegistrationController
tasks.description: Add @SpringBootTest slice tests for RegistrationController mocking OnboardingServiceClient. Cover: valid request -> 201 with correct body; missing githubUserId -> 400; duplicate active user -> 409; client throws connectivity error -> 503.

stories.summary: Onboarding request initialisation — internal endpoint in onboarding-service
stories.description: Implement an internal REST endpoint in <backend/onboarding-service/> that user-service calls synchronously after creating the user profile. The endpoint receives userId, correlationId, and roleKeys, creates an onboarding_request with state REQUESTED, transitions it to IN_PROGRESS, and creates three OnboardingStep rows (IDENTITY_CORRELATION, GITHUB_TEAM_PROVISIONING, JIRA_GROUP_PROVISIONING) each with state PENDING. Provider target resolution against group_mapping_rule is deferred to Phase 3; for Phase 2 the step rows are created with provider_target_id = null. The endpoint returns the new requestId and the step list. The endpoint path is /api/v1/internal/onboarding-requests and must not be exposed to the public API gateway.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Valid internal call creates request and steps
acceptanceCriteria.given: a POST to /api/v1/internal/onboarding-requests with a valid userId and correlationId
acceptanceCriteria.then: an onboarding_request with state IN_PROGRESS and three OnboardingStep rows with state PENDING are persisted and the response body contains the requestId and steps[]

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Returned steps list has all three step types
acceptanceCriteria.given: a successful internal call
acceptanceCriteria.then: it contains exactly IDENTITY_CORRELATION, GITHUB_TEAM_PROVISIONING, and JIRA_GROUP_PROVISIONING

acceptanceCriteria.given: a POST to /api/v1/internal/onboarding-requests with no userId
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is HTTP 400 with the standard error envelope

tasks.description: Add xyz.catuns.onboarding.service.api.dto.OnboardingInitRequest (userId UUID @NotNull, correlationId UUID @NotNull, roleKeys List<String>) and OnboardingInitResponse (requestId UUID, state String, steps List<StepSummaryDto>).

Add @Service xyz.catuns.onboarding.service.service.OnboardingInitialisationService with a @Transactional initialise(OnboardingInitRequest) method. Create OnboardingRequest (state=IN_PROGRESS, source=SELF_REGISTRATION, correlation_id from request). Create three OnboardingStep rows by resolving step_type_id from OnboardingStepType for IDENTITY_CORRELATION, GITHUB_TEAM_PROVISIONING, and JIRA_GROUP_PROVISIONING. Use OnboardingDomainService for state transitions.

Add @RestController xyz.catuns.onboarding.service.api.OnboardingInternalController with @PostMapping("/api/v1/internal/onboarding-requests"). Call OnboardingInitialisationService and return HTTP 201 with OnboardingInitResponse.

Add @DataJpaTest or @SpringBootTest slice tests using Testcontainers verifying that a valid call creates exactly one OnboardingRequest and three OnboardingStep rows with correct step types and PENDING state.

stories.summary: GET /api/v1/me — current user profile with optional includes
stories.description: Implement the GET /api/v1/me endpoint in <backend/user-service/>. The endpoint resolves the authenticated user from the bearer token by extracting the githubUserId claim, looks up the corresponding user_profile, and returns the profile response. When the query parameter include=identities is present, the response includes the github and atlassian identity blocks. When include=onboarding is present, the service calls onboarding-service to fetch the latest onboarding_request summary. Both includes can be requested together. The response shape must match the example in <docs/rest-api-reference.md> section 2 exactly. Return 401 when the token is missing and 404 when no user_profile exists for the resolved githubUserId.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a valid bearer token containing a githubUserId claim that maps to an existing user_profile
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is HTTP 200 with userId, displayName, primaryEmail, status, and roles[] populated correctly

acceptanceCriteria.given: a valid bearer token and an ExternalIdentity row for GITHUB exists for the user
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response includes a github object with userId, login, and email fields

acceptanceCriteria.given: a valid bearer token and an active onboarding request exists for the user
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response includes an onboarding object with requestId and state fields

acceptanceCriteria.given: a valid bearer token whose githubUserId does not match any user_profile
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is HTTP 404 with the standard error envelope

tasks.description: Add xyz.catuns.onboarding.user.security.JwtPrincipalExtractor that reads the githubUserId claim from the Spring Security Authentication principal. Wire it into UserProfileController to resolve the current user on each request.

Add MeResponse with userId, displayName, primaryEmail, status, roles, github (GitHubIdentitySummary), atlassian (AtlassianIdentitySummary), and onboarding (OnboardingSummary) fields. Make github, atlassian, and onboarding nullable to support partial includes.

Add @RestController xyz.catuns.onboarding.user.api.UserProfileController with @GetMapping("/api/v1/me"). Parse the include query param as a Set<String>. Call UserProfileService to load the profile and conditionally fetch identity or onboarding data based on the include set.

Add @Service xyz.catuns.onboarding.user.service.UserProfileService with a getMe(UUID githubUserId, Set<String> includes) method. Load UserProfile via ExternalIdentityRepository. When identities is in includes, load ExternalIdentity rows. When onboarding is in includes, call OnboardingServiceClient to fetch the latest OnboardingRequest summary.

Add getLatestOnboardingForUser(UUID userId) to OnboardingServiceClient. The onboarding-service must expose a corresponding GET /api/v1/internal/onboarding-requests/latest?userId= endpoint returning the most recent request state and requestId. Implement that endpoint in onboarding-service.
Add tests covering: base profile without includes; profile with include=identities when GitHub identity exists; profile with include=onboarding; unknown user -> 404; missing token -> 401.

stories.summary: Correlation ID propagation and MDC request tracing
stories.description: Wire correlation ID generation, propagation, and MDC logging across user-service and onboarding-service. A new UUID correlation ID is generated by user-service at registration and stored on both the outbox_event and the onboarding_request. The ID must be forwarded on all internal HTTP calls via the X-Correlation-ID request header. A servlet filter in each service must extract X-Correlation-ID from inbound requests and store it in MDC under the key correlationId so that all log lines within a request include it. This applies to all /api/v1 endpoints, not just registration.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a successful POST /api/v1/registrations
acceptanceCriteria.then: correlationId is a non-null UUID

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Inbound X-Correlation-ID is stored in MDC
acceptanceCriteria.given: an HTTP request arrives at user-service with header X-Correlation-ID: <uuid>
acceptanceCriteria.when: any log line is emitted during request processing
acceptanceCriteria.then: the MDC value for correlationId matches the header value

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: MDC is cleared after request completes
acceptanceCriteria.given: a request has been processed and the servlet filter has run its finally block
acceptanceCriteria.when: MDC is inspected after the response is sent
acceptanceCriteria.then: the correlationId key is absent from MDC

tasks.description: Add xyz.catuns.onboarding.user.filter.CorrelationIdFilter implementing OncePerRequestFilter. In doFilterInternal: read X-Correlation-ID header (or generate a new UUID if absent), store in MDC under correlationId, call the filter chain, then remove from MDC in a finally block. Register as a @Bean with highest precedence.

Add the equivalent CorrelationIdFilter in xyz.catuns.onboarding.service.filter using the same logic. Ensures that internal calls from user-service propagate the correlation ID into onboarding-service logs.
Update logback configuration in each service to include %X{correlationId} in the log pattern so correlation IDs appear on every structured log line without manual interpolation in application code.

Add MockMvc tests in user-service verifying: inbound X-Correlation-ID is echoed in the response header; a request without X-Correlation-ID generates a new UUID and includes it in the response; MDC is cleared between requests.
